title: 'Suspicious item' found at RAF Lossiemouth as site locked down by police

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Police raced to the base at at RAF Lossiemouth in Moray (Image: PA) A Scottish RAF base has been locked down after reports of the discovery of a suspicious package. Bomb squads raced to the base at RAF Lossiemouth in Moray after receiving the alarm at 11.25am on Thursday, March 12. Surrounding areas and roads have been cordoned off by police as a result of the ongoing incident. Residents in nearby homes have also been evacuated as a precaution. There are road closures on the B9135 at its junction with the A941, the A9135 at its junction with Muirton Road and Typhoon Road at its junction with B9315.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: A RAF spokesperson said: "I can confirm that during a routine security search at RAF Lossiemouth, a suspicious package was found. EOD has been notified a cordon is in place, and we are working with civilian agencies to resolve the issue." The force further confirmed the incident at Lossiemouth "is not part of the previously advertised exercise activity". Personnel at the base have been taking part in Exercise Agile Warrior this week. In a press release issued last week, the RAF said: "Exercise Agile Warrior 26; the RAF’s long-planned major homeland defence training activity launches this week. The exercise is designed to rigorously test the RAF’s ability to protect the United Kingdom in an increasingly contested and volatile global security environment and forms a critical step to ensuring the RAF remains ready to "fight tonight" in defence of the nation."
